markdown_projection.note: Per feedback_no_default_md_files and CX-908/CX-914, machine-readable JSON contract is authority; Markdown projection is on-demand only and is not generated by default.

spec_trace.roadmap_pointer: Kernel reset brief Week 4 Local Model + Memory V0 + Build-Rules Full Establishment fold, expanded by operator on 2026-05-18 to (a) ship all 9 inference research-stack techniques at PRODUCTION tier (LoRA hot-swap, KV caching, Activation Steering, Refusal Vector, CAA, Abliteration, MoD, Self-Speculative Decoding, Subquadratic architectures), (b) absorb full HBR enforcement wiring + missing tooling (visual debugger, backend manipulation/navigation, non-hijacking GUI interaction, swarm-agent harness, ModelManual currency, Codex+role-protocol HBR linkage), (c) defer sandbox architecture to refinement-phase landscape scan rather than pre-locking Docker or native, and (d) build Memory V0+ (retrieval capsule + self-improvement loop) on top of shipped FEMS V1.
spec_trace.roadmap_add_coverage: KERNEL-004 is the four-cluster fold packet: Cluster A wires HBR rules into PACKET_ACCEPTANCE_MATRIX, builds the visual debugger and backend manipulation surface, lands non-hijacking automation, the swarm-agent harness, ModelManual currency CI, and the Codex+role-protocol HBR linkage; Cluster B runs a landscape scan across 10+ sandbox candidates (Docker, Podman, nerdctl/containerd, Firecracker, gVisor, WSL2, Bubblewrap, Wasmtime, Deno, Handshake-native jail) and lands the chosen SandboxAdapter as a single abstraction shared by model-written code, local-model inference, validation runners, and the swarm-agent harness; Cluster C boxes a LocalModel inside Handshake via a ModelRuntime trait (engine TBD via research: llama.cpp/llama-cpp-2, candle, mistral.rs) and ships all nine inference techniques at PRODUCTION tier with explicit feasibility resolution for Mixture-of-Depths and Subquadratic architectures plus a distillation pipeline (EventLedger replay -> distilled LoRA) and a cloud-lane parity bridge for BYOK APIs and official CLI adapters; Cluster D lands the typed MemoryCapsule retrieval primitive, a promotion-gated self-improvement loop targeting a single measurable check, and FEMS V1 feedback integrations (bitemporal indexing, outcome feedback, pinned core memory, hygiene manager); Cluster X preserves Role Mailbox, executor routing, MT loop control, session crash recovery, and session observability spans from the historical WP-1-* stub set.

draft_scope.intent:
- What: KERNEL-004 is the largest WP in project history. It folds 30 historical stubs into a single coherent packet that (1) wires the existing HBR registry into hard build-time/handoff-time enforcement and adds the tooling the HBR pillars assume exists (visual debugger, backend manipulation/navigation surface, non-hijacking GUI interaction, swarm-agent test harness, ModelManual currency CI, Codex+role-protocol linkage), (2) selects and lands a sandbox architecture via a landscape scan rather than pre-locking Docker, (3) boxes a LocalModel inside Handshake through a ModelRuntime abstraction and ships an Inference Research Lab covering nine techniques at PRODUCTION tier with a distillation pipeline and cloud-lane parity, and (4) builds Memory V0+ (retrieval capsule + promotion-gated self-improvement loop) on top of the already-shipped FEMS V1 runtime. Per operator-binding decision on 2026-05-18, all nine inference techniques ship at PRODUCTION tier even though MoD and Subquadratic architectures may require an engine choice outside stock llama.cpp.
- Why: Each cluster on its own would touch the same kernel, sandbox, model, memory, and HBR surfaces. Splitting them into separate WPs would force three or more sequential migrations of the same product code, each one redoing the spec enrichment, the activation review, and the sandbox/runtime choice. The max-fold is operator-locked because the architecture decisions in clusters B and C and the enforcement decisions in cluster A are mutually entangled.
- Product goal: Handshake (Product) gains the ModelRuntime primitive, the SandboxAdapter primitive, the MemoryCapsule retrieval surface, the SelfImprovementLoop loop primitive, the DiagnosticsPanel and Inference Lab product surfaces, the ProcessOwnershipLedger, and the HBRGate enforcement surface, all wired through KERNEL-001 EventLedger, KERNEL-002 KernelActionCatalogV1/WriteBoxV1, KERNEL-003 ValidationRunner+PromotionGate, and FEMS V1.
- Operator goal: A solo operator can run governed local-model sessions inside Handshake without depending on LM Studio or Ollama, attach LoRAs and activation-steering vectors at runtime, run a bounded self-improvement loop against a single measurable check with operator-review promotion, see visual debugger output from any GUI run, spawn multiple governed concurrent sessions safely, and trust that every WP closure mechanically enforces all 23 HBR rules.
- No-context goal: This stub is intentionally stub-shaped. Refinement before activation is required to lock the sandbox architecture from the landscape scan, lock the first ModelRuntime engine, resolve MoD and Subquadratic engine-path feasibility, and apply the spec-v02.186 enrichment bundle bump before any microtask authoring begins.
- Post-research (2026-05-18): operator decisions locked - MoD deferred to spec Phase 3 roadmap [ADD v02.186] (NOT a stub, NOT a new WP); Subquadratic at full feature parity (multi-quarter); multi-engine adapter pattern (LlamaCppRuntime + CandleRuntime); 3-sandbox-adapter pattern (WSL2Podman + WindowsNativeJail + Docker); EAGLE-3 ship-at-GA; Abliteration offline-only; self-improve target = validator PASS rate on HBR test-packet corpus.
draft_scope.scope_sketch:
- IN_SCOPE:
  - Cluster A (HBR enforcement + tooling): A.1 enforcement wiring (packet hydration reads HANDSHAKE_BUILD_RULES.json, auto-emits PACKET_ACCEPTANCE_MATRIX rows, hbr-matrix-check.mjs gov-check sub-check, validator-scan integration), A.2 visual debugger (in-app Diagnostics panel + headless capture API, renderer console error scan HBR-VIS-002, capture matrix HBR-VIS-003), A.3 backend manipulation/navigation surface (programmatic state inspection, mutation routed through SessionBroker + KernelActionCatalogV1, trace projection API HBR-VIS-004), A.4 non-hijacking GUI interaction (headless/hidden-window mode HBR-QUIET-001, automation-path negative-test framework HBR-QUIET-002, kernel_process_lifecycle Postgres table HBR-QUIET-003), A.5 swarm-agent harness (kernel_swarm_test_harness for N concurrent governed sessions HBR-SWARM-001..004), A.6 ModelManual currency (HBR-MAN-001..003), A.7 Codex + role-protocol linkage (new CX-* anchor naming HBR as build-time/handoff-time gate authority).
  - Cluster B (Sandbox architecture): B.1 landscape scan across 10+ candidates (Docker Desktop, Podman, nerdctl/containerd, Firecracker microVM, gVisor, WSL2 namespaces, Bubblewrap, Wasmtime/WASM, Deno sandbox, Handshake-native Rust+OS-primitives jail) scored on Windows host parity, GPU pass-through, install friction, maintenance burden, security guarantees, swarm-agent concurrency cost, license risk, model-loading I/O performance; B.2 SandboxAdapter kernel API (single abstraction shared by model-written code, local-model inference, validation runners, swarm-agent harness; promotion via KERNEL-003 ValidationRunner -> PromotionGate -> authority); B.3 migration path from KERNEL-003 Docker (Docker stays as adapter under chosen architecture).
  - Cluster C (LocalModel + Inference Research Lab at PRODUCTION): C.1 ModelRuntime abstraction (Rust trait + adapter pattern inside KERNEL-001 ModelAdapter; first engine TBD by research; candidates llama.cpp via llama-cpp-2 crate, candle, mistral.rs; per-model profile durable authority via KERNEL-002 WriteBoxV1), C.2 inference research stack with all 9 techniques at PRODUCTION tier (LoRA hot-swap, KV caching, Activation Steering, Refusal Vector, CAA, Abliteration, MoD with HARD RISK, Self-Speculative Decoding, Subquadratic with HARD RISK), C.3 distillation pipeline (EventLedger replay -> teacher/student PEFT distillation -> distilled LoRA mountable into ModelRuntime), C.4 cloud lane parity (BYOK API adapters OpenAI/Anthropic; official-CLI bridge adapter Claude Code/Codex CLI; lane normalization per HBR-INT-005).
  - Cluster D (Memory V0+ on top of FEMS V1): D.1 retrieval capsule (bounded MemoryCapsule artifact, capsule generation policy per task type, capsule audit log per HBR-INT-006), D.2 self-improvement loop (Karpathy autoresearch one-iteration pattern; promotion-gated; operator review required), D.3 FEMS feedback loop integration (bitemporal indexing, outcome feedback, pinned core memory, hygiene manager).
  - Cluster X (Cross-cutting preservation): Role Mailbox, executor routing/claim/lease, MT loop control, MT executor, session crash recovery/checkpointing, session observability spans/FR.
- OUT_OF_SCOPE:
  - Any work outside the 30-stub fold boundary.
  - Creative modules (image/video/LoRA/scene production product surfaces) - KERNEL-005..008 reserved territory.
  - KERNEL-005, KERNEL-006, KERNEL-007, KERNEL-008 scope.
  - Week-5+ work beyond the four-cluster fold.
  - SQLite anywhere (CX-503R) including as a fallback, test fixture, or compatibility path.
  - Operator-owned adult-production compliance, paperwork, consent records, venue requirements, publishing rules.
  - Multi-tenant cloud-style billing, quota, or external account management.
  - Federated multi-Handshake-instance authority.
  - Provider-wrapping integrations that embed LM Studio or Ollama as runtime authority (ExternalEngineImport allowed as a non-authoritative pass-through).
- KEY_OPEN_QUESTIONS (deferred to refinement):
  - Q-SANDBOX-1: First engine choice for ModelRuntime (operator pick after landscape scan).
  - Q-SANDBOX-2: Sandbox architecture pick (operator pick after landscape scan).
  - Q-MoD-ENGINE: Which engine path can host MoD models at PRODUCTION tier on Windows + Rust embedding? (research output; may escalate to signature BLOCKER).
  - Q-SUBQUAD-ENGINE: Which engine path can host subquadratic architectures (Mamba/RetNet/RWKV) at PRODUCTION tier on Windows + Rust embedding? (research output; may escalate to signature BLOCKER).
  - Q-DISTILL-CORPUS: What governed-session data is allowed as distillation training corpus? (operator privacy + scope decision).
  - Q-SELF-IMPROVE-TARGET: What is the first measurable check the self-improvement loop targets? (operator strategic choice).
  - Q-VIS-DEBUG-ENGINE: Playwright vs CDP-direct vs Tauri-native devtools for visual debugger? (research output then operator pick).
  - Q-PROCESS-LEDGER-SCOPE: Does ProcessOwnershipLedger cover ALL Handshake-spawned processes or only sandbox/model processes? (boundary decision).
  - Q-MODELMANUAL-LOCATION: Where does the in-product ModelManual live in source? (new product surface; needs Section 10 spec authority + path).
draft_scope.acceptance_criteria:
- AC-001: Stub-level only - full AC list deferred to refinement and the official packet contract. The minimum-viable activation criteria below define when this stub can be promoted to a signed packet.
- ACTIVATION-AC-001: A refinement document exists with the four-cluster scope locked and the 30-stub preservation map complete with one-sentence intent preservation per folded stub.
- ACTIVATION-AC-002: Sandbox architecture landscape scan is complete with at least 10 candidates scored against the eight axes (Windows host parity, GPU pass-through, install friction, maintenance burden, security guarantees, swarm-agent concurrency cost, license risk, I/O performance).
- ACTIVATION-AC-003: All 9 KEY_OPEN_QUESTIONS are either answered with operator-recorded decision or explicitly scoped-deferred with a target resolution path.
- ACTIVATION-AC-004: Spec enrichment bundle bump from master-spec-v02.185 to master-spec-v02.186 is authored, reviewed, and active, covering the six enrichment topics (Section 4 LLM Infrastructure, Section 3 Local-first Infrastructure, Section 5 Security & Observability, Section 6 Mechanical Integrations, Section 10 Product Surfaces, Section 12 EOF Appendices).
- ACTIVATION-AC-005: B-MoD-SUBQUAD-FEASIBILITY is resolved (either a PRODUCTION-tier engine path is identified for each of MoD and Subquadratic, or operator approves a formal scope-reduction decision recorded in the refinement).
- ACTIVATION-AC-006: USER_SIGNATURE is captured in the refinement and propagates into the packet contract.
- ACTIVATION-AC-007: Microtask plan exists with at least cluster-level MT decomposition and a final count within the 155-210 estimate range; MT IDs are authored AFTER spec enrichment + open question resolution.
- ACTIVATION-AC-008: Research basis is recorded with sources checked, relevant patterns found, reuse opportunities, rejected options, selected approach, risks, mitigations, and validation plan per GLOBAL-RESEARCH-048..056.
- ACTIVATION-AC-009: Red-team is complete per CX-585B with the seven listed risks plus B-MoD-SUBQUAD-FEASIBILITY explicitly attacked and minimum controls enforceable via packet acceptance criteria.
- ACTIVATION-AC-010: Foundation surfaces in main (KERNEL-001 EventLedger/SessionBroker/ModelAdapter, KERNEL-002 KernelActionCatalogV1/WriteBoxV1/CRDT, KERNEL-003 Sandbox/ValidationRunner/PromotionGate, FEMS V1) are explicitly cited as reused substrate, not reimplemented.
key_open_questions: RESOLVED: Q-SANDBOX-1: First engine choice for ModelRuntime - multi-engine adapter pattern per operator E-3 (LlamaCppRuntime default + CandleRuntime for hook-requiring techniques).
RESOLVED: Q-SANDBOX-2: Sandbox architecture - 3-adapter pattern per operator SANDBOX decision (WSL2PodmanAdapter default + WindowsNativeJailAdapter strict + DockerAdapter compat).

DEFERRED_TO_SPEC: Q-MoD-ENGINE: No production path on Windows+Rust in 2026; MoD moved to spec Phase 3 roadmap addition per operator E-1 (NOT a stub, NOT a new WP, NOT in KERNEL-004 scope as a technique).
RESOLVED: Q-SUBQUAD-ENGINE: CandleRuntime (Mamba2 + RWKV) per operator E-2; full feature parity scope accepted with multi-quarter timeline.
PENDING_OPERATOR: Q-DISTILL-CORPUS: What governed-session data is allowed as distillation training corpus? (operator privacy + scope decision).

RESOLVED: Q-SELF-IMPROVE-TARGET: Validator first-pass PASS rate on fixed ~30-item HBR test-packet corpus; editable surface = ModelManual capsule text + retrieval policy params; safeguards = 60/20/20 train/dev/holdout encrypted + multi-metric promotion floor + Goodhart sentinel + HBR-SWARM-002 loop counter.
RESOLVED: Q-VIS-DEBUG-ENGINE: Playwright via CDP against WebView2 (primary) + tauri-driver as CI fallback per RW-4.
PENDING_OPERATOR: Q-PROCESS-LEDGER-SCOPE: Does ProcessOwnershipLedger cover ALL Handshake-spawned processes or only sandbox/model processes? (boundary decision).
PENDING_OPERATOR: Q-MODELMANUAL-LOCATION: Where does the in-product ModelManual live in source? (needs Section 10 spec authority + path).
draft_scope.risks_unknowns:
- R-1 Scope-consuming-product: This is the largest WP in project history. A WP this large risks the same failure mode the reset brief escaped, where the harness consumes the product. CONTROL: hard scope limit at the 30-stub-fold boundary; refinement explicitly forbids adding scope outside the folded stubs; spec enrichment is documented and bounded as a single coherent bundle bump so it does not quietly grow.
- R-2 PRODUCTION-tier research-frontier risk: The inference research stack contains research-frontier techniques (MoD, Subquadratic) at PRODUCTION tier; these may have no PRODUCTION-tier engine path in 2026. CONTROL: research workstream 3 must resolve this before signature; if no path exists, escalate B-MoD-SUBQUAD-FEASIBILITY for operator scope decision.
- R-3 Sandbox-architecture-flip-orphans-KERNEL-003: Flipping sandbox architecture away from KERNEL-003 Docker could orphan KERNEL-003 sandbox code. CONTROL: refinement requires the KERNEL-003 sandbox lane preserved as an adapter under the chosen architecture, with explicit migration MTs.
- R-4 Spec-enrichment-becomes-its-own-project: Enrichment of this size could itself grow into a project. CONTROL: enrichment is a single coherent bundle bump (master-spec-v02.186), pre-reviewed by operator, applied in one governed pass; no incremental enrichment churn.
- R-5 LM-Studio/Ollama-lockout: The no-LM-Studio/no-Ollama invariant could lock out the easiest local-model story. CONTROL: keep an ExternalEngineImport lane that lets the operator point Handshake at a model file path or a local OpenAI-compatible endpoint without depending on those apps as runtime authority.
- R-6 Visual-debugger-parallel-mutation-path: A visual debugger built without coupling to KERNEL-002 KernelActionCatalogV1/WriteBoxV1 could become a parallel mutation path. CONTROL: all backend manipulation paths go through KernelActionCatalogV1; no debug-only direct DB writes.
- R-7 Self-improvement-overfit: The self-improvement loop overfits to its measurable check. CONTROL: target is a fixed test corpus; promotion requires operator review; loop-counter HBR-SWARM-002 caps runaway; the metric must be validated against a held-out corpus before promotion.
- B-MoD-SUBQUAD-FEASIBILITY (cross-listed as blocker): If research shows MoD or Subquadratic cannot ship at PRODUCTION tier in any candidate engine, operator must approve scope reduction or accept a long timeline. May block scope lock.
- Risk: SQLite reappears via test fixtures or compatibility paths. Mitigation: Postgres/EventLedger only per CX-503R; activation must verify zero SQLite surfaces are introduced.
- Risk: Adult-production scope creep - Memory/Distillation could tempt absorbing performer records or consent paperwork. Mitigation: scope is identity + governance + inference only; consent paperwork stays operator-owned per GLOBAL-PRODUCTION-010..015.
draft_scope.governance_artifact_stance:
- Machine-readable governance contracts are primary per CX-908 and CX-914.
- Markdown is not generated by default per feedback_no_default_md_files; Markdown projections are on-demand only and never carry authority.
- This stub must produce a `.contract.json` projection before it is treated as registered governance inventory.
- This stub is not executable until it becomes an official signed packet with refinement, packet contract, microtask contracts, work branch, worktree, and activation approval.
- Refinement scope is intentionally larger than usual because KERNEL-004 folds 30 historical stubs across HBR enforcement, sandbox architecture, local-model boxing, inference research lab, and Memory V0+. Per GLOBAL-PRESERVATION-009..013, the 30 folded intents are preserved (not selectively extracted) and carried forward via the stub_fold_preservation_map in the refinement contract. Do not shortcut the open-question pass or the landscape scan.

assumptions_to_attack: A stub is planning metadata, not execution authority.
Stub draft_scope is intentionally pre-research and will be replaced by hydrated refinement content.
All 9 inference techniques at PRODUCTION tier is operator-binding scope; research may reveal feasibility blockers for MoD and Subquadratic.
Sandbox architecture decision is deferred to landscape scan, not pre-locked.
Spec enrichment v02.186 is required before signature; current spec is insufficient depth for cluster C and parts of A/B.
minimum_controls: Require official packet contract before Coder or Validator launch.
Refinement must surface MoD/Subquadratic engine-path feasibility before signature; if no PRODUCTION path exists, escalate B-MoD-SUBQUAD-FEASIBILITY for operator scope decision.
Refinement must preserve all 30 folded stub intents (GLOBAL-PRESERVATION-009..013).
Spec enrichment is a single coherent bundle bump; no incremental drift.
JSON contract is authority; Markdown is on-demand projection only (CX-908, CX-914, feedback_no_default_md_files).
